headline: Occupational Health Services Cover Only 22% of Malaysian Workers
context: Most Malaysian workers lack access to workplace health services despite Occupational Safety and Health Act requirements.

cards.big: 78% of Malaysian workers have no access to occupational health services in 2025
cards.sub: Small and medium enterprises employing 7.3 million workers are least covered

cards.big: OSHA 1994 requires workplace health programs but DOSH inspects only 8% of workplaces annually
cards.sub: Enforcement gap leaves millions of workers without mandated health protections

cards.big: Occupational disease claims rose 34% from 2022 to 2025 with musculoskeletal disorders leading
cards.sub: Underreporting means actual occupational illness prevalence is far higher

cards.big: Finland's occupational health system covers 92% of workers through mandatory employer provision
cards.sub: Universal workplace health reduces sick days and disability claims significantly

cards.big: Workers who get sick from their jobs should not have to fight for the healthcare they deserve
Should all employers be required to provide basic occupational health services regardless of company size
